title: myroxylon grayi
intro: Myroxylon grayi, also known as Palo Santo, is a tropical tree native to Central and South America, particularly in Ecuador and Peru, belonging to the legume family (Fabaceae). The plant's resinous wood and leaves have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits associated with Palo Santo include reducing stress and anxiety, promoting relaxation, and potentially alleviating symptoms of respiratory issues such as asthma and bronchitis. Herbal preparations of Palo Santo include essential oils, extracts, and teas, often used in aromatherapy and traditional medicine to promote well-being and relaxation.
